parsed_review.summary:
Read-only review of CP00-782 (RP25.P07.M05.S11-S20, risk A, 10 units, micro "Permission And Audit Binding"). The descriptor-verified production_ready flag is well supported and the pack correctly remains production_ready=false / runtime_ready=false pending this gating review (manifest, construction-inspection PENDING, adjudication, and claude-review-result are mutually consistent — no premature readiness claim).

Implementation: MIGRATION_PLATFORM_CP782_PACK_BINDING (registry.js:451) and CP782_REQUIREMENTS (registry.js:2086) match the baseline exactly — deliverable_counts {failure_recovery:3,implementation:1,hermes_evidence:2,fixture:1,test:2,security_audit:1}, required_section_rows=PERMISSION_MATRIX_FAILURE_ROWS.slice(10,20) (the 10 tail rows lock_conflict→hermes_failure_evidence, no overlap with CP781's slice(0,10)). service.js:1765-1820 adds caseSet/descriptor/hermes/claude/handoff following the established pattern; handoff targets CP00-783/RP25.P07.M05.S21. validators.js:2329-2413 mirror CP781 with correct substitutions and enforce descriptor_only, no runtime execution, no source-system runtime, no permission-decision/cross-tenant/audit-body leakage, and authority guards.

Tests/validation: model.test.js:991-1054 assert binding, empty coverage/descriptor errors, authority boundary (claude_is_final_approval=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true, runtime_opening_pack_id=CP00-783), runtime-closed flags, and latest contract projection with all historical projections preserved. command-evidence shows all 10 expected commands plus extras at exit 0 (npm test 3030/3030 pass, migration 82/82 pass, git diff --check clean), and closeout-pack-plan:validate confirms "queue starts after CP00-782". contracts/migration-platform-contract.json has no "valid":false entries; latest_projection/authority_boundary/no_write_attestation are consistent.

Boundaries/authority: descriptor-only, no real data, writes_product_state=false; Hermes packet is descriptor-only review/receipt evidence (emits_runtime_receipt=false), not embedded product runtime; Claude stays read-only non-final; runtime opening is deferred to CP00-783 and no runtime/enterprise-trust claim is made. Readiness auth preflight passed with valid JSON (non-zero stdout) — no auth-failure/0-byte/malformed evidence to reject. No P0/P1/P2 issues. One P3 data-quality defect: a corrupted phantom path in the manifest's unrelated-files list (benign; authoritative changed-file-scope.json is correct and the real contract is in active scope). Verdict: PASS_WITH_FINDINGS, non-blocking.

findings.title: Corrupted path string in manifest unrelated_dirty_files_preserved list
findings.details: worktree_transition_policy.unrelated_dirty_files_preserved contains "ontracts/migration-platform-contract.json" (missing the leading 'c'). The real contracts/migration-platform-contract.json is the pack's primary deliverable and is correctly classified as an active implementation ref in the same manifest (active_pack_required_untracked_files, line 205) and in the authoritative changed-file-scope.json (active_pack_implementation_refs). The corrupted entry points to a non-existent path, so it cannot be preserved/excluded and has no effect on commit staging (stage_only_pack_scope will still stage the real contract). It is a copy/paste corruption in a required pack artifact, not a scope error. Separately, changed-file-scope.json lists docs/closeout-packs/cp00-782/ and docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md in both untracked_implementation_files and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved, but active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted is [] so no active file is excluded.
findings.recommendation: Fix the typo to "contracts/migration-platform-contract.json" or, more correctly, remove it from unrelated_dirty_files_preserved entirely since the contract is an active pack deliverable. Optionally tighten the manifest generator so the same path cannot appear in both active and unrelated lists. No re-run of validation is required for closeout.
findings.post_review_disposition: Acknowledged as non-blocking data-quality cleanup. Does not affect pack closeout: changed-file-scope.json is authoritative and correct, the contract is staged as an active implementation ref, all validations pass, and no real-data/write/authority boundary is impacted.
